title: Poa walteri
taxonomy: Poa walteri is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Poa, which includes several other species of grasses. The genus Poa is part of the subfamily Pooideae, which is a large group of cool-season grasses. Poa walteri is native to Australia and is often found in grasslands and open woodlands. This species is closely related to other Poa species found in Australia and New Zealand.

intro: Poa walteri, also known as Walter's meadow-grass or Walter's grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Poa walteri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Poa walteri is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues, digestive problems, and skin conditions.
